seo.title: Lamb Shoulder - Elana Vermaak, here it comes - Marinda Kook

html:
To get a really nice lamb shoulder cooked just right you need time on your side, Elana wrote to me that her shoulder isn't deboned, and where she lives there aren't many big shops where she can buy all sorts of things. Well, for a lamb shoulder you cook it the way its mother would have. Nothing too much or fancy. Keep it true to its flavour.

  • set your oven to 180 degrees Celsius
  • salt and pepper your shoulder generously
  • in the bottom of an oven dish cut 3 onions (I love onions), 3 carrots into rounds, 8 cloves of garlic (just with the skin still on)
  • on top of the onions and carrots place about 4 sprigs of rosemary
  • now rub your shoulder well with the juice of one lemon and olive oil
  • place the shoulder on the bed of vegetables and pour in half a cup of white wine and half a cup of water
  • put the lid on or cover with tinfoil. Forget about the shoulder in the oven for an hour and a half
  • peel small potatoes and small onions. After an hour and a half add the potatoes and small onions to the leg in the dish.
  • now remove the lid/tinfoil and bake in the open oven for another 1 hour.

The shoulder should now be melt-in-the-mouth tender and browned.
